title: Split Folio Statement
prompt:
Create a production-ready Split Folio Statement using React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. Present an editorial statement split across two folio panels: the left carries a numbered premise and the right carries the response. A button toggles which half is emphasized, moving a single mint rule between the two panels and changing the reading order summary. Use #F4F5F6 around a white Bento card, #ECECEE borders, 22px corners, soft shadow, #111111 text, and #4A4A4F support copy. Mint is the only bright accent and no panel may use a gradient.

Use a semantic button with aria-pressed plus a polite live region that announces the emphasized folio. Hover lifts the button 2px over 180ms; press compresses it 1px over 90ms. The mint rule must use a stiffness 350/damping 28 spring, and the newly emphasized line must transition in opacity and y over 180ms. Enter and Space follow the same action. Keep both statements visible, so the meaning does not depend on animation. Give the control a 44px target, clear 3px dark focus ring, logical tab order, and AA contrast.

At 320px, stack the folios without losing their numbered labels; in a detail preview, they may use a two-column composition. Prefer transform and opacity. Do not use timers, remote media, local storage, random values, network calls, or dynamic code. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by moving the rule instantly or fading it within 120ms. Include complete TypeScript React client code and a runnable pure CSS approximation with suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* Suggested HTML: <section class="uiwiki-folio"><article>01 Make the evidence visible.</article><article>02 Make the decision reversible.</article></section> */
.uiwiki-folio{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(2,minmax(0,1fr));gap:12px}.uiwiki-folio article{min-height:128px;padding:16px;border-radius:18px;background:#f4f5f6;color:#111}.uiwiki-folio article:first-child{border-bottom:4px solid #53e8d4}.uiwiki-folio article:hover{transform:translateY(-2px)}@media(max-width:360px){.uiwiki-folio{grid-template-columns:1fr}}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.uiwiki-folio article{transition:none}}

demoCode:
(() => {function Demo(){const [r,setR]=React.useState(false);const f=[['01','Make the evidence visible.'],['02','Make the decision reversible.']];return
{f.map((x,i)=>
{x[0]}{x[1]}{(r?i===1:i===0)&&}
